description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone fragment with a rough, porous surface and a mix of tan, brown, and off-white coloration. The stone displays a granular texture with visible inclusions and small cavities, suggesting a sedimentary or volcanic origin. One side features a patch of white mineralization, while the other sides are mottled with darker brown and yellowish tones. The stone has a broken, jagged edge and a more weathered, rounded opposite side. A black alphanumeric marking and a measurement scale are visible on one face, indicating it may have been cataloged for study or construction. The overall appearance suggests it has been exposed to the elements, with some surface weathering and discoloration.
description: A rough, tan and brown stone fragment with a porous, granular texture and visible mineral inclusions. One side is marked and measured, showing signs of weathering and surface irregularity.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the stone, highlighting its porous, granular texture and tan coloration with scattered dark inclusions.
description_view_1: This angle reveals the stone's thickness and a prominent white mineral patch, along with a black alphanumeric marking and a measurement scale for reference.
description_view_2: Here, the stone's jagged, broken edge is visible, along with the measurement scale and catalog marking, emphasizing its irregular shape and mottled surface.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the stone's more angular, pointed end and the extensive white mineralization, with less visible marking and a rough, uneven surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a loose barrier stone
attribute_pattern: Mottled and speckled with irregular patches of white mineralization and scattered dark inclusions; no regular or artificial patterning.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly eroded with visible surface pitting and discoloration, indicating moderate exposure to weather.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smooth, man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; could be partially embedded in soil or surrounded by vegetation.
